Title: employees.create IPC handler does not emit employee.hired bus event
Description: Architectural invariant #11 gap. employees.create handler (handlers.ts L1766) performs the durable write but does NOT emit a bus event. Renderer employee-list caches cannot auto-invalidate on hire; callers must rely on React Query mutation onSuccess fallbacks which are brittle under E2E/agent flows. Same pattern step f closed for goals/projects/tickets — extends that closure into employees namespace.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Invoke employees.create IPC handler with valid companyId+roleId+name. 2. Observe no bus.emit call in the handler body. 3. grep apps/desktop/src/main/ipc/handlers.ts for 'employee.hired' returns 0 sites.
Expected Result: Handler emits an 'employee.hired' bus event after successful hire with actorId=HUMAN_USER_ID + full Employee snapshot (invariant #11 contract)
Actual Result: No bus.emit in handler; renderer caches stale after IPC-driven hire
Environment: Repo HEAD a4dc24e; vitest 106/106 companies tests green but inv#11 gap is out-of-scope for companies-handlers.test.ts
Resolution: FOLLOWUP-P1-extended atomic: employeesCreate now emits employee.hired with EmployeeHiredPayload (employeeId + companyId + roleId + level + name + title + hiredAt); HUMAN_USER_ID actor; try/catch swallow; dev-mode unwired warn. Renderer useEmployeeEventSync hook (use-employees.ts) subscribes and invalidates ['employees' + 'orgchart']. Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-18-team-x-phase-5.6-m-c-followup-p1-extended.md
Verified By: invariant-11-emit-handlers.test.ts 'employees.create (BUG-009)' describe — 5 assertions + cross-cutting HUMAN_USER_ID sweep extension

Title: employees.fire IPC handler does not emit employee.fired bus event
Description: Architectural invariant #11 gap. employees.fire handler (handlers.ts L1806) performs the durable employee archive + keychain cleanup but does NOT emit a bus event. Renderer caches for org chart + employee lists cannot auto-invalidate on fire. Same FOLLOWUP-P1 pattern as BUG-009.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Invoke employees.fire IPC with existing employeeId. 2. Observe no bus.emit in handler body L1806. 3. grep handlers.ts for 'employee.fired' returns 0 sites.
Expected Result: Handler emits 'employee.fired' bus event with firedAt timestamp + companyId + employeeId snapshot captured BEFORE the fire (since subsequent getById returns null)
Actual Result: No bus emit; renderer caches stale
Resolution: FOLLOWUP-P1-extended atomic: employeesFire now emits employee.fired with EmployeeFiredPayload (employeeId + companyId + roleId + level + name + title + firedAt). Snapshot-before-drop — roleId/level/name/title captured from getById BEFORE the employeesRepo.delete so audit-view chips + renderer optimistic removals retain the identifier after the row is gone. Mirrors company.deleted / goal.deleted / project.deleted snapshot discipline. isSystem refuse-path preserved (no emit on system-agent fire attempts). Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-18-team-x-phase-5.6-m-c-followup-p1-extended.md
Verified By: invariant-11-emit-handlers.test.ts 'employees.fire (BUG-010)' describe — 5 assertions incl. seedSystem refuse-path + snapshot-before-drop verification

Title: tickets.attachFile + tickets.detachFile IPC handlers do not emit bus events (known-deferred from step f)
Description: Architectural invariant #11 gap. KNOWN deferred per step f nextStep note explicitly scoped OUT ('Attachment lifecycle (tickets.attachFile/detachFile) is deferred to a later FOLLOWUP-P1-extended milestone before M-D attachment UI needs cache invalidation'). Filed here so the tracker surfaces the gap alongside BUG-009/010 for a single FOLLOWUP-P1-extended atomic.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Invoke tickets.attachFile with valid ticketId + fileId. 2. Observe no bus.emit in handler body L3952. 3. grep handlers.ts for 'ticket.attached' or 'ticket.attachmentAdded' returns 0 sites.
Expected Result: Both handlers emit ticket.attachmentAdded / ticket.attachmentRemoved bus events with ticketId + fileId + companyId + actor snapshot (invariant #11)
Actual Result: No bus emit on either handler; ticket detail panel attachment list stale after IPC-driven attach/detach
Resolution: FOLLOWUP-P1-extended atomic: ticketsAttachFile now emits ticket.attachmentAdded (TicketAttachmentAddedPayload with attachmentId/ticketId/companyId/fileId/attachedBy/attachedAt). ticketsDetachFile now emits ticket.attachmentRemoved with snapshot attachmentId captured via listByTicket + fileId match BEFORE the detachByFile drop; null when no match (no-op detach still emits for optimistic-path reconciliation). Both handlers thread companyId via a ticketsRepo.getById fetch (doubles as phantom-id guard). Renderer useTicketEventSync widened: subscribes to both attachment events + adds narrow invalidation keyed per-ticket via ['ticket-attachments' + payload.ticketId]. Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-18-team-x-phase-5.6-m-c-followup-p1-extended.md
Verified By: invariant-11-emit-handlers.test.ts 'tickets.attachFile (BUG-011)' + 'tickets.detachFile (BUG-011)' describe blocks — 8 assertions incl. no-op detach emits attachmentId null + phantom ticket rejected pre-emit + event-sync-hooks.test.ts useTicketEventSync attachment-events assertions
